title: Cubby Desk with Drawer
summary: A small desk built from cleats, aprons, and a single drawer — a plywood or MDF top with iron-on edge banding, designed to pair with a matching cubby hutch above.
description:
This desk is built from the inside out: cleats attach to the back apron first, then side aprons attach to the cleats, so the whole base frame goes together before the top ever comes into the picture. Predrilling every screw hole with a countersink bit matters more here than on thicker stock — the aprons are working with relatively thin material, and a split board at this stage means rebuilding the base.

The top glues onto the frame with back and side edges kept flush, and the top gets attached to the frame through the cleats from below — genuinely fastener-free on the visible top surface. Finish the exposed top edges with iron-on edge banding for a solid-wood look on a plywood top, or skip that step entirely and use MDF if you're painting, since paint hides MDF's edge in a way stain never will.

The single drawer is sized to fit its opening with a 1/2" gap on either side for slide clearance, and dividers cut from 1x3 stock can be added inside for pencils and supplies — a genuinely worthwhile add-on since a desk drawer without dividers becomes a junk drawer almost immediately. Mount the drawer inset 3/4" from the front of the desk, then true up the drawer face last, nailed and glued directly to the drawer box front. **This desk is designed to pair with a matching hutch** — the source treats the hutch as a companion piece with its own separate plans, built and mounted above the desk once the desk itself is complete.

tools.note: Cutting the plywood top to size.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Cut to fit your actual opening minus 1/2" per side for slide clearance.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut one back apron at 26", two side aprons at 18-3/4", two small trim cleats at 3-1/2", four drawer box sides/front/back at 17" and two drawer dividers at 8" from the 1x4. Cut two cleats at 18" and four legs at 29-1/4" from the 2x4. Cut one top at 29" from the 3/4" plywood or MDF, cut piece. Cut one drawer bottom at 17" from the 1/4" plywood, scrap. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to two 8 ft 1x4 and two 8 ft 2x4. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw — several parts here are within half an inch of each other and are easy to mix up later.

steps.title: Attach the cleats to the back apron
steps.body: Screw the cleats to the back apron with glue and 2-1/2" or 3" screws, predrilling holes with a countersink bit to keep the wood from splitting.

steps.title: Attach the side aprons
steps.body: Screw the side aprons to the cleats.

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Glue the top to the frame, keeping the back and side edges flush. Screw the cleats to the tabletop from below with 2" screws. Apply iron-on edge banding to the exposed edges for a solid-wood look, or skip if using MDF for a painted finish.

steps.title: Add the legs
steps.body: Attach the legs with 2-1/2" or 3" screws and glue.

steps.title: Build and install the drawer
steps.body: Build the drawer box with 2" screws and glue, sized to fit the opening with a 1/2" gap on either side for slides. Add dividers if desired. Attach the drawer bottom. Mount the drawer into the desk using the slides, inset 3/4" from the front.

steps.title: Attach the drawer face and finish
steps.body: Line up the drawer face with the drawer and nail on with finish nails and glue. Add a knob or handle. Sand and finish the desk.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Small kids desk with a single drawer
